data.title: Google says AI Plus subscription to expand to more countries
data.description:
• Google (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) said late on Tuesday that its AI Plus subscription plan will be expanding to 40 countries, as it looks to keep boosting adoption of its viral image generation and editing model, informally known as Nano Banana.
  • The plan includes access to higher limits of the aforementioned model, as well as added access to its video generation model, Veo 3 Fast, directly inside the Gemini app. Other benefits of the subscription include access to its Whisk and Flow Gemini AI image and filmmaking tools; Gemini access built into Gmail, Docs, Sheets and more; Higher limits in NotebookLM; and 200 GB of storage across Photos, Drive and Gmail.
  • The subscription can be shared with five other family members, Google added.
  • Added countries include Angola, Bangladesh, Benin, Bolivia, Ukraine, Vietnam and more. Prices vary by country, but the plan costs $19.99 per month in the U.S.
  • Nano Banana has helped boost usage of Google's Gemini chatbot app, which surpassed OpenAI's ChatGPT as the most downloaded free app on the App Store earlier this month.
  • Google shares fell 0.8% in late morning trading.

data.title: Axon Enterprise rated Overweight in new coverage at Piper Sandler
data.description:
Axon Enterprise (NASDAQ:AXON), best known for its Taser devices, is undergoing a transformation that positions it as a leader in a much broader public safety technology market, according to new research from Piper Sandler. Analyst James Fish late Tuesday initiated coverage with an Overweight rating, citing the company’s innovation pipeline, cross-sell opportunities and strong visibility from recurring revenue.

Axon (NASDAQ:AXON) has expanded its offerings well beyond non-lethal weapons, according to the Sept. 23 report. Years of product development and strategic acquisitions have added body cameras, in-car Fleet systems, connected workflows and intelligence software to its portfolio, boosting the company’s total addressable market (TAM) from about $50 billion in 2022 to $129 billion today.

Roughly 70% of Axon’s (AXON) business now comes from government clients, particularly state and local agencies. Fish highlights this exposure as a strength, pointing to steady budgets and rising investment in law enforcement efficiency. He described the sector as experiencing a “physical security renaissance,” similar to the cybersecurity boom more than a decade ago.

Axon’s (AXON) expansion potential remains significant, with an estimated two billion frontline workers worldwide but only about one million currently using its products. U.S. penetration in state and local markets is less than 15%. A key driver of future growth, the report says, will be upselling existing customers to higher-end Officer Safety Plans that bundle devices with AI-powered software. Piper Sandler estimates this cross-sell and upsell opportunity could generate $4.5 billion in annual recurring revenue, with Axon’s (AXON) Draft One AI assistant alone representing a $600 million market today.

Product refresh cycles should also contribute to growth. The recent launches of Taser 10 and Body 4 have been strong, and major upgrades are expected in the coming years (Taser in 2027–2028, body cameras in 2027 and Fleet in 2028–2029). With more than 95% of customers on subscription contracts, Axon’s (AXON) backlog and ARR provide what Fish calls “visibility for miles,” with potential for revenue to sustain growth above 25% annually through 2027.

While acknowledging that Taser sales growth has slowed and Axon trades at a premium valuation, Fish described it as “one of the few hypergrowth assets in a growth-scarcity environment,” supported by low estimates, strong innovation, and upcoming product refreshes. Piper Sandler set a price target of $893 for Axon (AXON), based on 17.5 times projected 2027 sales.

data.title: CNO Financial to buy minority stake in Janus Henderson's Victory Park Capital
data.description:
Janus Henderson Group (NYSE:JHG) said Wednesday that life and health insurer CNO Financial Group (NYSE:CNO) will buy a minority interest in its majority-owned alternative asset manager, Victory Park Capital Advisors.

In addition, CNO (NYSE:CNO) will provide a minimum of $600M in capital commitments to new and existing VPC investment strategies.

"This collaboration reinforces our shared belief in the long-term potential of asset-backed private credit markets and further deepens Janus Henderson and VPC’s insurance presence," said Janus Henderson CEO Ali Dibadj. "By partnering with like-minded institutions, we are continuing to strengthen our ability to deliver client-led solutions aligned with our strategy to amplify our strengths.”

Janus Henderson (NYSE:JHG) bought VPC last year in a move that expanded its private credit and institutional capabilities.

CNO (CNO) manages 3.2M policies and $37.3B in total assets as of June 30, 2025.

data.title: Cidara jumps after expedited late-stage trial plan for flu therapy
data.description:
Cidara Therapeutics (NASDAQ:CDTX) added ~15% on Wednesday after the biotech announced an accelerated timeline for a Phase 3 registrational trial designed to evaluate its experimental flu therapy CD388.

The update followed an End-of-Phase 2 meeting the company conducted with the U.S. FDA to discuss the future submission of a biologics license application for the antiviral based on a single Phase 3 trial for the injectable.

Based on the FDA feedback, San Diego, California-based Cidara (NASDAQ:CDTX) has decided to expand the trial to include adults aged more than 65 years in addition to those older than 12 years with immunodeficiency or high-risk health conditions.

The decision will double the population that CD388 can potentially be marketed to, from roughly 50M to 100M individuals in the U.S., Cidara (NASDAQ:CDTX) noted. The trial's enrollments are expected to start by the end of this month in the Northern Hemisphere and will continue into the Spring of 2026 in the Southern Hemisphere.

The revised timeline indicated a six-month acceleration from its prior plan, Cidara (CDTX) said, adding that the study will include an interim analysis after the Northern Hemisphere flu season.

“Older adults receive less protection from vaccines because the immune system naturally declines with age. Including them in our trial not only addresses a critical unmet need but will also accelerate enrollment,” CEO Jeffrey Stein noted.

After the update, Guggenheim analyst Seamus Fernandez raised his price target on CDTX from $70 per share to $167, the highest on Wall Street, according to Bloomberg data. “We categorize the FDA discussion as strong support and validation, providing a potential upside case of a broader patient population that, in our view, supports more rapid recruitment and execution,” Fernandez wrote with a Buy rating on the stock.

data.title: Business uncertainty over revenue rises, while employment uncertainty dips in September: Atlanta Fed
data.description:
Business uncertainty about revenue growth (smoothed): 3.83% in September vs. 3.77% in August, according to an Atlanta Fed survey released on Wednesday.

Employment growth uncertainty (smoothed): 4.16% vs. 4.23% prior.

Developing... check back for updates.

data.title: These stocks could be impacted by Tito's Handmade Vodka expanding into tequila
data.description:
Tito's Handmade Vodka made the first acquisition in the company's history by entering an agreement to snap up a majority stake in LALO Tequila, which was noted to be one of the fastest-growing tequila brands in the United States.

LALO Tequila is a premium, additive-free tequila crafted in Los Altos de Jalisco, Mexico, using just fully mature Highland Blue Weber agave, Champagne yeast, and deep well water. The brand offers LALO Tequila Blanco, bottled at 40% ABV, and LALO High Proof Tequila, at 54% ABV, both designed to deliver a clean, bright expression of agave with no aging or masking additives. LALO Tequila has generated a strong reputation with tequila purists.

"At Tito's, we've always said if you're going to do something, do it well," said Tito Beveridge, founder of Tito's Handmade Vodka. Beveridge said he has known the LALO founders for a long time. "They keep it simple. They do things the right way, not the flashy way," he observed.

The newly formed relationship with LALO will provide the tequila brand with strategic sales support and access to an expanded distribution network to accelerate its scale from a local name to one with global reach.

Evercore ISI analyst Robert Ottenstein said Tito's foray into tequila is incrementally negative for Diageo (NYSE:DEO), Brown-Forman (NYSE:BF.A) (NYSE:BF.B), and others, who will have to compete against an already fast-growing brand that will now have access to Tito’s distribution network, strategic support, and likely deep pockets.

Tito's Handmade Vodka is still privately owned and controlled by founder Bert "Tito" Beveridge, through Fifth Generation, Inc.

data.title: Rivian faces US Auto safety investigation over seat belt issues in delivery vans
data.description:
The U.S. Office of Defects Investigation on Wednesday opened an investigation about issues regarding seat belt performance of Rivian’s (NASDAQ:RIVN) electric delivery vans.

The office of Defects Investigation opened a preliminary evaluation to check the scope of potential problem for approximately 17,198 Rivian vans produced between 2022 and 2023.

The authority has received six reports of potential failures of the driver’s front outboard seat belt anchorage system. In multiple reports, the seat belt steel braided cable frayed or broken, leaving the occupants unrestrained in the event of a collision, said the authority in its document.

The investigation will focus on the integrity of the seat belt assembly, installation method, and potential design/manufacturing changes and deficiencies.

Rivian did not immediately respond to a Seeking Alpha request for comment.

Earlier this month, The EV-maker reportedly recalled over 24,000 R1S and R1T vehicles from the 2025 model year due to a software defect that may cause the Hands-Free Highway Assist system to misidentify lead vehicles.

data.title: Former energy secretary Rick Perry's data center REIT launches IPO roadshow
data.description:
Data center REIT Fermi (FRMI) announced on Wednesday the launch of a roadshow for its proposed IPO of 25M shares, expected to be priced between $18 and $22 per share.

The data center REIT was co-founded by the former U.S. Energy Secretary Rick Perry and the co-founder of Quantum Energy (OTCPK:QREE), Toby Neugebauer.

The company develops electric grids that deliver highly redundant power at gigawatt scale, required to create next-generation artificial intelligence.

A registration statement relating to the securities has been filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission, but has not yet become effective.

Also called Fermi America, the company intends to grant the underwriters a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 3.75M shares at the IPO price.

The company intends to list its shares on the Nasdaq Global Select Market and the London Stock Exchange under the ticker symbol FRMI.

Net proceeds are intended to be used to support the development of business, secure personnel, and increase financial flexibility, and for general corporate purposes.

data.title: Packaging Corp. of America rated Overweight in new coverage at J.P. Morgan
data.description:
Packaging Corp. of America (NYSE:PKG) is well positioned to capitalize on structural changes in the corrugated container industry, according to financial-services firm J.P. Morgan. Analyst Detlef Winckelmann on Wednesday initiated coverage with an Overweight rating, highlighting PKG’s market leadership, favorable supply-side dynamics and strong balance sheet.

Shares of PKG rose 1.4% by 10:41 a.m. ET Wednesday.

Although PKG is the nation’s third-largest box producer, Winckelmann noted it is considered the leader “on virtually every metric.” The U.S. corrugated sector, he argued, is undergoing a shift as competitors focus on value over volume, a strategy that could ultimately shrink capacity and allow PKG to capture market share.

“Peers International Paper (NYSE:IP) and Smurfit WestRock (NYSE:SW) are both under new management and have chosen to embark on value over volume strategies for their box businesses,” Winckelmann wrote. “The most likely outcomes of these new strategies, in our view, are peers losing box volumes to PKG and peer paper integration declining. Given this is undesirable, the most likely outcome seems to be mill capacity closures.”

With operating rates already at 94% to 95%, J.P. Morgan expects limited new supply and even potential net closures in the short to medium term. This could tighten the market despite demand levels that remain below historical averages.

PKG also benefits from financial flexibility. Following its $1.8 billion acquisition of Greif, the company’s net debt is about two times earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization. Winckelmann projects that figure will decline to around 1.3 times by 2026, creating room for shareholder returns. He estimates PKG could support annual buybacks of $500 million, roughly 2.5% of its market capitalization, by 2027 or sooner.

While PKG is the firm’s preferred U.S. pure play, J.P. Morgan also maintained a bullish stance on Smurfit WestRock (NYSE:SW). Winckelmann sees approximately 65% of its ebitda exposed to U.S. market tailwinds, with management guiding $800 million to $1.2 billion in incremental box business ebitda over the next five years. Consensus forecasts, he noted, imply far lower gains, creating what he called “a low bar to beat expectations.” At about 6.5 times enterprise value-to-ebitda, Smurfit (SW) trades below International Paper (NYSE:IP), which is valued above eight times.

“PKG is uniquely placed to benefit from these dynamics and the valuation looks attractive, especially as our blue-sky scenario appears to be becoming increasingly likely,” Winckelmann concluded.

J.P. Morgan set a December 2026 price target of $242 a share, using an equal weighting of a one-year forward enterprise value-to-ebitda multiple valuation and a one-year forward free cash flow yield multiple valuation.

data.title: Copper futures jump as Freeport hit with Grasberg mine suspension impact
data.description:
Copper prices jumped more than 3% Wednesday after Freeport-McMoRan (FCX) detailed the significant production impact from the suspension of its Grasberg Block Cave mine in Indonesia following this month's deadly mud rush incident.

Production in the Grasberg minerals district - the world's second-largest copper mining complex - was stopped following the incident, and Freeport (FCX) said preliminary assessments indicate the impacts likely will result in the deferral of significant production in Q4 2025 and full-year 2026, with "a return to pre-incident operating rates potentially achieved in 2027."

Nymex copper futures (HG1:COM) shot up immediately on the update, reversing an earlier decline, and recently traded +3.2% to $4.739/lb.

Freeport's (FCX) disclosure is the latest disruption to the copper industry, after Hudbay Minerals (HBM) said it is shutting operations at a mill at its Constancia mine site in Peru due to ongoing political protests.

While Freeport-McMoran (FCX) sinks to its lowest in nearly two months, -10.6%, Southern Copper (SCCO), Hudbay (HBM) and Teck Resources (TECK) rallied +9.5%, +6.1% and +5.1%, respectively.

In London, Glencore (OTCPK:GLCNF) (OTCPK:GLNCY) shares surged as much as 3.5% while Antofagasta (OTC:ANFGF) added 8.7%.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:CPER), (NYSEARCA:COPX), (OTC:JJCTF)
